text: The reading ‘forty-two’ in the Hebrew text is clearly a scribe’s mistake for ‘twenty-two’, which is preserved in the Septuagint Greek. This is evident both from IV Kg. 8.26, and from the fact that Joram was only forty when he died.

This passage seems to correct the impression we should otherwise derive from IV Kg. 9.27, that Ochozias died of the wound he received during his escape at Jezrahel. Jehu, it appears, killed him at Mageddo; ‘in Samaria’ is simply a way of describing the northern kingdom, as in III Kg. 13.32.
